So assuming that this is a fishing rod, I'm not sure if the reel is a part of a rod or not. But you cast it and then hopefully you feel something tugging on the end of it.  And then you can wind it up and that way you see what you ended up catching.

So a fishing reel is, I'm guessing it actually is part of the fishing rod. The fishing rod, that part you just showed me where you will cast it. And then once you want to reel it back in, like you felt the tug, for example, you'll spin the lever on the side, I guess, until you get the rod back.

Okay. So three hole punch is a tool, often gray, that you'll use to punch three holes into a piece of paper  that you might want to do that for like putting something through those holes.  Maybe like if you're putting them in a binder, for example.

Okay, so a three hole punch is a tool that you use when you want to punch holes into a piece of paper, usually like one, maybe towards the, on the side of the paper with one hole at the top, one in the middle, one at the bottom, often because you want to place it in a binder that has like three rings, each hole corresponding to a ring.

And so it revolves around so you can go from letter to letter.

A hand mixer is a device that you use to whisk food, probably while baking, where you'll, on the top you'll hold it where there's a handle, and then on the side you have the crank that you can use to hold it.

A pocket radio is a device that allows you to play music, and it fits in the palm of your hand as well as in your pocket, so it's portable and it has the antenna which connects to the signal, and it allows you to connect to the different stations. You can slide something on the side to change it from FM to AM, and then you can also slide between channels and the scale, which might be related to the frequency.

A blood pressure cuff is something that you'll tie onto your upper arm, and then you'll usually like go to the doctor, and the doctor will pump it, and you'll feel like it tightening on your arm, and then there's a thing, a circular device on the other end that will measure your blood pressure.

A blood pressure cuff is a medical device that you'll... There's an arm strap where you'll attach it to your upper arm, and it holds in place thanks to a Velcro on it, and you can, or somebody else, sometimes a doctor, can press on... I don't want to describe that piece, but there's one piece that you can press on and then another piece coming out of the strap that will then give you a reading on this circular piece of your blood pressure.

A stethoscope is to measure somebody's heartbeat. You'll put the piece that detects it against their heart, I believe, and then usually another person, the doctor, will then put this type of apparatus where they can listen into your heartbeat. So a shoehorn is something that you use to slide your feet into shoes, especially if you like tight shoes. Sometimes they're used especially for dress shoes and that it will, it's like this long tool with like a hook at the end. And so the hook, you put it on like the heel or the backside of the shoe and then slide your foot in.
